title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in robotic lunar landing missions, which has sparked both excitement and confusion within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI. This deal marks a significant transaction in the satellite manufacturing and asset-financing sector.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract with a Japanese company to supply a satellite bus for a technology demonstration mission. This deal marks another international collaboration for Apex in the satellite manufacturing sector.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract from the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights the growing demand for hypersonic technology in defense applications.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 25 commercial players
summary: Space Command is conducting a classified wargame that will include 25 commercial players to improve collaboration and understanding of commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations between Space Command and the commercial sector.

title: TransAstra aims to move 100-ton asteroid to stable orbit for processing
summary: TransAstra is studying the feasibility of moving a 100-ton asteroid into a stable near-Earth orbit for processing and manufacturing. The project aims to establish a robotic research outpost in the Earth-moon system.

title: Solar array deal sheds more light on South Korea’s defense constellation
summary: South Korea's defense satellite constellation plans are advancing with a new solar array supply deal, targeting a first demonstrator launch by late 2027. The agreement highlights the country's push to enhance national security capabilities through space assets.

title: Frontier justice: navigating the future legal landscape for private actors in space law
summary: The article discusses the evolving legal landscape for private actors in space law, drawing parallels with historical frontier concepts. It explores challenges and opportunities in regulating private space activities.

title: China signals new target for 2027 asteroid deflection test
summary: China has selected a new target near-Earth asteroid for its first planetary defense kinetic test mission, planned for launch in December 2027.

title: NASA grappling with planetary science funding shortfall
summary: NASA's planetary science program faces a funding shortfall despite avoiding steep cuts proposed earlier, forcing strategic decisions on mission continuations.

title: NASA peers inside an asteroid | Space photo of the day for March 19, 2026
summary: NASA has utilized advanced imaging to examine asteroid Bennu samples, revealing extensive networks of cracks within the rock particles. This discovery provides new insights into the asteroid's composition and history.

title: Explosive daytime fireball may have dropped meteorites on Ohio — here's where to look and what to do if you find one
summary: A rare daytime meteor exploded over Ohio with the force of 250 tons of TNT, potentially dropping meteorites. Experts have provided guidance on where to look and what to do if fragments are found.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: AI cyberattacks could potentially hijack satellites and cause catastrophic collisions within the next two years, posing a severe threat to Earth's orbit. Experts warn that current defenses may not be adequate to prevent a 'satellite apocalypse.'

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit on March 20
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' on March 20. The launch will be livestreamed for the public.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: Two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes will be available on BBC iPlayer in the UK. NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a $50 voucher for a two-year subscription.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is set to roll out the Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of its planned April 1 launch. The event will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: 'At the edge of what we thought possible': Astronomers find extremely rare star from ancient universe
summary: Astronomers discovered an extremely rare, iron-deficient second-generation star from the ancient universe, offering insights into how early stars enriched the cosmos. This finding provides evidence of stellar processes in the early universe.

title: Astronauts complete prep for new ISS solar array on 1st NASA spacewalk in 10 months
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Chris Williams completed preparations for a new ISS solar array during the first U.S. spacewalk in 10 months. The spacewalk on March 18, 2026, marked a significant step toward upgrading the station's power system.

title: How to watch 'Invincible' Season 4 online and from anywhere
summary: Prime Video's animated superhero series 'Invincible' Season 4 premieres today. Fans can watch it online via Prime Video.

title: SpaceX fires up next-gen 'V3' Starship for 1st time ahead of April launch (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted a static fire test of the first stage of its next-generation 'V3' Starship on March 16. This marks a key milestone for the larger and more powerful rocket variant ahead of its planned April launch.

title: NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking
summary: NASA will broadcast the launch and docking of the Roscosmos Progress 94 cargo spacecraft, which is delivering supplies to the International Space Station. The unpiloted spacecraft is set to launch on March 22 and carry three tons of food, fuel, and other essentials.

title: Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class
summary: NASA's newest astronaut candidate class, selected in 2025, has been named 'the Platypi' and is training at Johnson Space Center for future missions to the ISS, Moon, and Mars.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure and shaking, confirming it can withstand launch conditions. The mission remains on track for an upcoming launch.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Australia's Central Australia experienced significant greening due to heavy rainfall in February and March 2026, transforming its desert landscape. This environmental change highlights the impact of weather patterns on arid regions.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission is led by Catherine O'Connell-Cooper from the University of New Brunswick, Canada.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: NASA's Landsat 8 captured a clear image of Mayon volcano in the Philippines on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows using infrared observations. PHIVOLCS reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds during this period.

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from military service to a NASA Pathways engineering internship at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in engineering environments through the program.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The news lists various upcoming space industry events and gatherings scheduled in 2026, including the Maryland Space Business Roundtable, SIA Annual Leadership Dinner, Artemis Suppliers Conference, and others.

title: NASA’s Hubble Unexpectedly Catches Comet Breaking Up
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured the rare event of comet K1 (C/2025 K1 ATLAS) breaking apart. The findings were published in the journal Icarus.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2 in preparation for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This marks another milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine launches planned, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects (NEOs). The mission concept combines multiple strategies for detecting and deflecting potential asteroid threats.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for human missions to the Moon and Mars, showcasing advancements at its Starfactory facility. The company is transforming steel production into spacecraft manufacturing.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: A new crew arrived at the International Space Station (ISS) in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January. The month was marked by crew arrivals and scientific activities aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket set to dominate the launch schedule. The post highlights the busy week ahead for the space industry.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase after Flight 11 on October 13, 2025. This follows a 147-day gap since the last flight.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha rocket (FLTA007 'Stairway to Seven') after a 10-month pause, marking its return to flight operations.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Baikonur Cosmodrome's Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The repairs clear the path for Russia's critical resupply mission.

title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced major changes to the Artemis program to restore its momentum, though key details are still being developed.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details or events are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports the spacecraft is operational and communicating again.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of industry events to present follow-up actions to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator, with launch planned for January 2027 aboard an Ariane 6 rocket. The spacecraft's 26 ultra-sensitive cameras are being exposed to extreme temperatures and vacuum conditions to validate functionality before deployment.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6, a new costing software, on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic systems and other technologies. This initiative aims to foster hands-on learning and innovation in space exploration.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75, targeting a launch no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission next year. This deal marks Apex's entry into the Japanese space market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with the updated version expected to launch this summer. The changes were planned before a recent anomaly, and the company is accelerating their implementation.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round. The company aims to reduce global reliance on satellite infrastructure.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another step in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights NanoAvionics' growing partnerships within the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for commercial space launches.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet growing demand, but policymakers have the opportunity to accelerate growth according to industry groups.

title: Nvidia Unveils New Hardware to Unlock Orbital Data Centers
summary: Nvidia has introduced new hardware designed to enable orbital data centers, marking a significant step toward space-based computing infrastructure. The announcement highlights the growing interest and technological advancements in deploying data centers in Earth's orbit.

title: Canadian Launchers and Spaceport Get Funding Boost
summary: Canada will invest in a spaceport and three young rocket systems to enhance its sovereign launch capabilities. This funding boost aims to strengthen the country's space industry.

title: Leonardo’s Missile-Defense Demo in Ukraine Aims To Be a Blueprint for Other Nations
summary: Leonardo is conducting a missile-defense demonstration in Ukraine, aiming to establish a model for other nations. The company seeks to become a major distributor of space-enabled missile defense systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare at an event in London, four years after Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted evolving military strategies and technologies.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a new proposal with the FCC, asserting that its SkyTerra Next payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites will not cause interference. The company claims the plan aligns with existing L-band coordination agreements.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly supply solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea’s sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) national security constellation. Hanwha Systems is also involved in the project.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article discusses the critical importance of satellite communication (Satcom) terminal survivability in contested environments for U.S. military operations. It highlights the need for beyond-line-of-sight connectivity for command and control, mission execution, and safety-of-life communications.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic unveiled plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' satellites, offering one-meter resolution and daily mapping capabilities. The constellation aims to enhance Earth observation services.

title: 9 New Faces for SATShow Week 2026
summary: SATShow Week 2026 announces nine new speakers, expanding its program to include more military speakers with GovMilSpace's second-year participation.

title: Industry Roundtable With ’10 Hottest’ Executives
summary: Via Satellite interviewed executives from the '10 Hottest Companies in Satellite' for 2026 ahead of SATShow Week to gather their insights on industry trends and developments.

title: Driving Ubiquitous Connectivity: Multi-Orbit Satellite Architecture and Advanced User Terminals
summary: The article discusses the role of multi-orbit satellite architecture and advanced user terminals in achieving ubiquitous connectivity. It highlights the potential of this technology to enhance global communication networks.

title: Telesat’s Lightspeed Service Launch Slips to 2028
summary: Telesat's Lightspeed LEO constellation launch has been delayed to 2028 due to ASIC delays for satellite processors. The service was originally planned to start earlier.

title: Nvidia Launches New Chips for Space Missions
summary: Nvidia introduced new chips designed for space missions, targeting applications such as orbital data centers, geospatial intelligence, and autonomous space operations. The announcement highlights Nvidia's expansion into the space industry with support from companies like Aetherflux.
